message.content:
{
  "analysis": "The clean autotune job (PID 4838) is running in the background, and the terminal appears back at a prompt after the prior polling loop ended. The last visible autotune status was Trial 1 at about 31.9% with ETA around 10 minutes, and `/app/work/model_aut2.bin` was still 0 bytes because fastText autotune writes the final model at completion. I need to wait for autotune to finish, then verify the produced model size and local test accuracy, remove the `.vec` if present, and copy a qualifying model to `/app/model.bin`.",
  "plan": "Start a single bounded polling loop for up to 15 minutes checking PID 4838 and `/app/work/model_aut2.bin`. When the process is finished and a nonzero model binary exists, print the autotune log tail, remove `.vec`, list model files, evaluate local accuracy with `/opt/fastText/fasttext test`, and if accuracy >=0.62 and size <150MB copy the model to `/app/model.bin`. If no valid model is produced, print a clear fallback message.",
  "commands": [
    {
      "keystrokes": "cat > /app/work/wait_eval_copy.sh <<'SCRIPT'
#!/bin/bash
set -u
PIDFILE=/app/work/autotune2.pid
LOGFILE=/app/work/autotune2.log
MODEL_BIN=/app/work/model_aut2.bin
MODEL_FTZ=/app/work/model_aut2.ftz
TESTFILE=/app/work/test.txt
FINAL=/app/model.bin
MAX_WAITS=30
WAIT_INTERVAL=30
MAX_SIZE=150000000
MIN_ACC=0.62

echo "=== Waiting for autotune to finish ==="
for i in $(seq 1 "$MAX_WAITS"); do
  pid=$(cat "$PIDFILE" 2>/dev/null || true)
  if [ -n "$pid" ] && [ -d "/proc/$pid" ]; then
    printf '(%s/%s) pid=%s running; ' "$i" "$MAX_WAITS" "$pid"
    tail -1 "$LOGFILE" 2>/dev/null || true
    sleep "$WAIT_INTERVAL"
    continue
  fi
  if [ -s "$MODEL_BIN" ] || [ -s "$MODEL_FTZ" ]; then
    echo "autotune finished and model file exists"
    break
  fi
  printf '(%s/%s) finished but no model file; ' "$i" "$MAX_WAITS"
  tail -1 "$LOGFILE" 2>/dev/null || true
  sleep "$WAIT_INTERVAL"
done

echo "=== Autotune log tail ==="
tail -40 "$LOGFILE" 2>/dev/null || true

echo "=== Clean/check model files ==="
rm -f /app/work/model_aut2.vec
ls -lh /app/work/model_aut2.* 2>/dev/null || echo 'No model files found'

if [ -s "$MODEL_BIN" ]; then
  MODEL="$MODEL_BIN"
elif [ -s "$MODEL_FTZ" ]; then
  MODEL="$MODEL_FTZ"
else
  echo 'No valid autotune model produced; fallback needed'
  exit 1
fi

SIZE=$(stat -c %s "$MODEL")
echo "Selected model: $MODEL size=$SIZE bytes"

echo "=== Evaluate local test accuracy ==="
/opt/fastText/fasttext test "$MODEL" "$TESTFILE" 2>&1 | tee /app/work/eval_aut2.txt || echo 'test failed'

echo "=== Parse accuracy ==="
awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {acc=$3+0; print "parsed_accuracy="acc; if (acc >= min && acc > 0) {print "accuracy_ok"; exit 0} else {print "accuracy_too_low"; exit 1}}' /app/work/eval_aut2.txt
code=$?  # In bash, this captures the awk exit code.

echo "=== Copy to /app/model.bin if size and accuracy OK ==="
if [ "$SIZE" -lt "$MAX_SIZE" ]; then
  if [ -s /app/work/eval_aut2.txt ] && awk -v min="$MIN_ACC" '/^P@1/ {acc=$3+0; if (acc >= min && acc > 0) {exit 0} else {exit 1}}' /app/work/eval_aut2.txt; then
    cp "$MODEL" "$FINAL"
    echo "copied to $FINAL"
    ls -lh "$FINAL"
    echo "final_size_bytes=$(stat -c %s "$FINAL")"
    echo "FINAL_OK"
  else
    echo "accuracy below $MIN_ACC; not copying"
    echo "AUTOTUNE_ACCURACY_TOO_LOW"
  fi
else
  echo "model size $SIZE >= $MAX_SIZE; not copying"
  echo "AUTOTUNE_SIZE_TOO_LARGE"
fi
SCRIPT
chmod +x /app/work/wait_eval_copy.sh
/app/work/wait_eval_copy.sh
",
      "duration": 600.0
    }
  ]
}
